researched_category.notes: Telegram is a cloud-based mobile and desktop messaging app. For developer/agent purposes, it provides multiple APIs including the Bot API for creating bot accounts that interact with users and groups programmatically.

one_liner.value: Telegram is a cloud-based messaging app that provides a free Bot API (HTTP-based) for creating bots to send/receive messages, manage groups and channels, and integrate with services.
one_liner.notes: Core.telegram.org describes three APIs: Bot API (simplified HTTPS), Telegram API (MTProto for custom clients), and Gateway API.

methods.notes: Bot API uses a bot access token (API key/bearer token) obtained from @BotFather. The Telegram API/MTProto uses api_id + api_hash (app credentials) plus phone-based user authentication (SMS code). The Gateway API uses a token-based authentication.

access.notes: Bot API is completely free. Anyone with a Telegram account can create bots via @BotFather with no approval or paid plan. The Telegram API/MTProto requires registering an application at my.telegram.org (free, requires a Telegram account). Gateway API is free to test. No paid plan or admin approval is required for any of the APIs.

summary.value: Telegram offers three documented public APIs. (1) Bot API: comprehensive HTTPS REST API with ~70+ methods covering messaging, groups, channels, inline mode, payments, games, polls, webhooks, and rich media. Fully documented at core.telegram.org/bots/api with a changelog. (2) Telegram API (MTProto): low-level API for building custom Telegram clients, with methods for all Telegram features. (3) Gateway API: for sending verification codes via Telegram.
summary.notes: The Bot API is the most relevant for agent toolkits. It is an HTTP-based REST API with extensive documentation, webhook support, and long polling for updates.

agent_interface.notes: Multiple community MCP servers exist: dryeab/mcp-telegram (247 GitHub stars, uses MTProto via Telethon), siavashdelkhosh81/telegram-bot-mcp-server, guangxiangdebizi/telegram-mcp, SmartManoj/Telegram-Bot-MCP, IQAIcom/mcp-telegram, and others. Composio also offers a hosted Telegram MCP toolkit with 18 tools. No official MCP server from Telegram.

rationale.value: Telegram is fully buildable as an agent toolkit today. The Bot API is a well-documented, free, public REST API with a simple bearer-token authentication. Multiple community MCP servers already exist and are in active use. The only consideration is whether to use the Bot API (simpler, bot-only interaction) or the Telegram API/MTProto (full user account access). For most agent use cases (sending messages, managing groups, channels), the Bot API is sufficient, self-serve, and free.
rationale.notes: No material blockers. The API is free, well-documented, and already has multiple MCP integrations working in production.

s1.quote: We offer three kinds of APIs for developers. The Bot API allows you to easily create programs that use Telegram messages for an interface. The Telegram API and TDLib allow you to build your own customized Telegram clients. You are welcome to use both APIs free of charge.
s1.title: Telegram APIs

s2.quote: The Telegram Bot Platform hosts more than 10 million bots and is free for both users and developers.
s2.title: Bots: An introduction for developers

s3.quote: Supported authentication methods: API bot access token. To generate your access token: 1. Start a chat with the BotFather. 2. Enter the /newbot command to create a new bot...
s3.title: Telegram credentials | Nodes | n8n Docs

s4.quote: In order to obtain an API id and develop your own application using the Telegram API you need to do the following: - Sign up for Telegram using an official application. - Log in to your Telegram core: https://my.telegram.org. - Go to 'API development tools' and fill out the form. - You will get basic addresses as well as the api_id and api_hash parameters required for user authorization.
s4.title: Creating your Telegram Application

s5.quote: Authorization is associated with a client's encryption key identifier: auth_key_id. ... To log in as a bot, follow these instructions.
s5.title: User Authorization

s6.quote: Telegram's Bot API itself is completely free — there are no per-message charges, no monthly API fees, and no limits on the number of bots you can create.
s6.title: Telegram Bot API Pricing: Is It Free? Full Cost Breakdown (2026)

s7.quote: The Bot API is an HTTP-based interface created for developers keen on building bots for Telegram.
s7.title: Telegram Bot API

s8.quote: Securely connect your AI agents and chatbots (Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, etc) with Telegram MCP or direct API to send messages, automate group chats, manage channels, and interact with users through natural language.
s8.title: Telegram MCP Integration for AI Agents | Composio

s9.quote: Control Telegram using LLMs through the Model Context Protocol (MCP). Built with Telethon, this server allows AI agents to interact with Telegram...
s9.title: MCP Telegram Server | Awesome MCP Servers

s10.quote: A powerful Model Context Protocol (MCP) server for seamless Telegram Bot API integration with intelligent message splitting, comprehensive error handling, and NPX support.
s10.title: GitHub - siavashdelkhosh81/telegram-bot-mcp-server

s11.quote: An MCP (Model Context Protocol) server for interacting with Telegram bots and channels using the Telegraf library.
s11.title: BrainDAO/mcp-telegram

s12.quote: A comprehensive Model Context Protocol (MCP) server for seamless Telegram Bot API integration
s12.title: GitHub - guangxiangdebizi/telegram-mcp

limitations: The dryeab/mcp-telegram server (247 stars) uses MTProto/Telethon for full user-account access, which requires api_id/api_hash and phone-based auth - this is more complex than the Bot API token approach. The Bot API approach (token-based) is simpler and more common for agent toolkits. No official MCP server from Telegram exists, but multiple community options are available.
